useCallback(\\n    (targetView: ViewMode) =>\\n      dispatch({ type: \\\"CLEAR_ADJUSTMENT_STATE\\\", view: targetView }),\\n    [],\\n  );\\n\\n  const handleScopeSelect = useCallback(\\n    (scope: \\\"global\\\" | \\\"local\\\") => {\\n      const { activeSessionDetail } = latestRef.current;\\n      if (!activeSessionDetail) {\\n        setAgentLoopStatus({ phase: \\\"idle\\\" });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      // For \\\"local\\\", no server call needed — rule already has exact selector\\n      if (scope === \\\"local\\\") {\\n        setAgentLoopStatus({ phase: \\\"idle\\\" });\\n        toast.success(\\\"Regel gilt nur für diesen Block.\\\");\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      // For \\\"global\\\", update rule selectors to block_type strategy\\n      setScopeMutation.mutate(\\n        { sessionId: activeSessionDetail.session.id, scope },\\n        {\\n          onSuccess: () => {\\n            queryClient.invalidateQueries({\\n              queryKey: orpc.rules.list.key(),\\n            });\\n            setAgentLoopStatus({ phase: \\\"idle\\\" });\\n            toast.success(\\\"Regel gilt jetzt global.\\\");\\n          },\\n          onError: () => {\\n            setAgentLoopStatus({ phase: \\\"idle\\\" });\\n            toast.error(\\\"Scope konnte nicht geändert werden.\\\");\\n          },\\n        },\\n      );\\n    },\\n    [queryClient.invalidateQueries, setScopeMutation.mutate],\\n  );\\n\\n  return {\\n    sectionRef,\\n    adjustmentAvailable: statusQuery.data?.available ?? false,\\n    adjustModeEnabled: isAdjustModeEnabled,\\n    activeSessionDetail: state.activeSessionDetail,\\n    activeSelection,\\n    activeAnchor: state.anchorByView[view],\\n    activeDraftMessage: state.draftMessageByView[view],\\n    activeSessionError: state.sessionError,\\n    activeSessionLoading: createSession.isPending,\\n    isSubmitting: appendMessage.isPending,\\n    isDiscarding: discardSession.isPending,\\n    showGuide:\\n      isAdjustModeEnabled &&\\n      !activeSelection &&\\n      !state.guideDismissedByView[view],\\n    replyVisible: state.replyVisibleByView[view],\\n    guideDismissed: state.guideDismissedByView[view],\\n    toggleAdjustMode,\\n    handleSelectionChange,\\n    handleDraftMessageChange,\\n    handleSubmitMessage,\\n    handleDiscardSession,\\n    clearCurrentAdjustmentState,\\n    setReplyVisible,\\n    setGuideDismissed,\\n    agentLoopStatus,\\n    handleScopeSelect,\\n  };\\n}\"}]"}

content.text:
Score this issue for PR #4 on confidence scale 0-100.

Issue: In `use-element-screenshot.ts`, the `capture` function is a plain async function, not wrapped in `useCallback`. The sibling hook `useBlockMarkup` (added in same PR) wraps its function in `useCallback`. This inconsistency means `capture` creates a new reference every render.

Scoring rubric:
- 0: False positive, doesn't stand up to scrutiny, or pre-existing
- 25: Might be real, might be false positive, stylistic without CLAUDE.md backing
- 50: Real but nitpicky or unimportant
- 75: Very likely real, important, directly impacts functionality or explicitly in CLAUDE.md
- 100: Definitely real, frequent impact, evidence confirms

To verify: run `gh pr diff 4` and check use-element-screenshot.ts for useCallback usage. Compare with use-block-markup.ts. Check if `capture` is used in any dependency arrays.

diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
index 4d86f03..4a85f1c 100644
--- a/CLAUDE.md
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -370,6 +370,74 @@ Claude Code entscheidet selbstständig, ob ein Agent Team gespawnt wird. Folgend
 
 ---
 
+## Tooling — Serena + RTK (MANDATORY)
+
+Zwei Optimierungen reduzieren Token-Verbrauch drastisch. **Native Claude Code Tools (Read, Grep, Glob) umgehen beide** — daher vermeiden.
+
+### Tool-Hierarchie (bindend)
+
+| Aufgabe | 1. Wahl (Serena MCP) | 2. Wahl (Bash → RTK) | NICHT verwenden |
+|---------|---------------------|----------------------|-----------------|
+| **Code lesen** | `find_symbol(name, include_body=true)`, `get_symbols_overview` | — | `Read`, `cat` |
+| **Code suchen** | `find_referencing_symbols`, `search_for_pattern` | — | `Grep`, `rg` |
+| **Code-Dateien finden** | `find_file`, `list_dir` | — | `Glob`, `find` |
+| **Code editieren** | `replace_symbol_body`, `insert_after_symbol` | `Edit`-Tool | `sed` |
+| **Nicht-Code lesen** (md, json, config, logs) | — | `cat`, `head` → RTK komprimiert | `Read` |
+| **Nicht-Code suchen** | — | `rg`, `grep` → RTK komprimiert | `Grep` |
+| **Git, Tests, Build** | — | Bash → RTK komprimiert | — |
+
+### Warum Serena für Code?
+
+Serena liest auf **Symbol-Ebene** — eine Funktion statt 500 Zeilen. Beispiele:
+
+```
+# Statt `cat button.tsx` (ganze Datei):
+get_symbols_overview("button.tsx")              → zeigt nur Exports/Struktur
+
+# Statt `cat use-adjustment-session.ts`:
+find_symbol("useAdjustmentSession", include_body=true)  → nur der Hook
+
+# Statt `grep -r "getAdjustViewLabel" src/`:
+find_referencing_symbols("getAdjustViewLabel")  → alle Referenzen mit Kontext
+```
+
+### Warum RTK für Nicht-Code?
+
+Der RTK-Hook greift **nur bei Bash-Tool-Calls** und komprimiert Output um 60-90%. Native Tools (Read, Grep, Glob) umgehen den Hook — RTK sieht sie nie.
+
+```
+# Automatisch vom Hook umgeschrieben:
+git status   → rtk git status
+cat README.md → rtk read README.md
+rg "TODO"    → rtk grep "TODO"
+```
+
+### Ausnahmen (native Tools erlaubt)
+
+- **`Edit`-Tool** — Serena und RTK haben kein vollständiges Äquivalent für alle Fälle
+- **`Write`-Tool** — Neue Dateien erstellen
+- **`Read` nur als Prerequisite vor `Edit`/`Write`** — Edit erfordert ein vorheriges Read
+- **`Read` für Bilder/PDFs** — nur native Read kann Binärdateien verarbeiten
+
+### Subagent-Prompts (WICHTIG)
+
+Beim Spawnen von Subagents die Tool-Hierarchie explizit mitgeben:
+
+> "Nutze Serena MCP Tools für Code-Operationen (find_symbol, get_symbols_overview, search_for_pattern, find_referencing_symbols). Nutze Bash (cat/grep/find) nur für Nicht-Code-Dateien (JSON, Markdown, Config). Vermeide native Read/Grep/Glob Tools."
+
+**Anti-Pattern:** "Nutze Bash statt native Tools" — das überspringt Serena komplett und ist bei Code-Operationen token-ineffizient.
+
+### RTK Meta Commands
+
+```bash
+rtk gain              # Show token savings analytics
+rtk gain --history    # Show command usage history with savings
+rtk discover          # Analyze Claude Code history for missed opportunities
+rtk proxy <cmd>       # Execute raw command without filtering (for debugging)
+```
+
+---
+
 ## Tooling — Entire.io Integration
 
 Dieses Repository nutzt `entire.io` um AI-Agent-Sessions zu tracken. Entire zeichnet automatisch alle Prompts, Reasoning und Tool-Ausführungen im Hintergrund auf.

# Testing Best Practices — Chat Exporter

> Verbindliche Referenz für alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** — es enthält die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.

## Tech-Stack

| Bereich | Tool | Version |
|---------|------|---------|
| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | — |
| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |

## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing

**Lies nur die Module, die für deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**

| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
|---------|-------------------|
| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
| **Code-Review durchführen** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
| **Test-Kategorien wählen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
| **Verfügbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |

---

## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gültig)

### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung

Tests prüfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.

```typescript
// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })

  const result = generatePreview(selection)

  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
})

// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
  generatePreview(createSelection())
  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
})
```

### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)

Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:

```typescript
test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
  // Arrange
  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }

  // Act
  const result = createSelection(overrides)

  // Assert
  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
})
```

### Given-When-Then (für komplexe Szenarien)

```typescript
test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
  /**
   * Given: Eine aktive Session für importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
   * Then: Bestehende Session zurückgegeben, keine neue erstellt
   */
})
```

### Tests als Dokumentation

Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`

---

## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)

| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |

Score this issue for PR #4 on confidence scale 0-100.

Issue: In `use-placeholder-rotation.ts`, inside the `setInterval` callback, a `setTimeout` is scheduled for the fade transition (150ms delay). The cleanup function (`clearTimer`) only clears the interval but not the inner timeout. If the component unmounts during those 150ms, setState calls fire on unmounted component.

Scoring rubric:
- 0: False positive, doesn't stand up to scrutiny, or pre-existing
- 25: Might be real, might be false positive, stylistic without CLAUDE.md backing
- 50: Real but nitpicky or unimportant
- 75: Very likely real, important, directly impacts functionality or explicitly in CLAUDE.md
- 100: Definitely real, frequent impact, evidence confirms

To verify: run `gh pr diff 4` and look at use-placeholder-rotation.ts for the setInterval/setTimeout pattern and whether the timeout ref is tracked in cleanup.

The issue correctly identifies dead code that violates the CLAUDE.md TDD protocol. ### Verification Findings **Type definition** (`use-adjustment-session.ts:26-33`): ```typescript export type AgentLoopStatus = | { phase: "idle" } | { phase: "thinking" } | { phase: "applying"; iteration: number } | { phase: "verifying"; iteration: number } | { phase: "asking_scope" } | { phase: "done" } | { phase: "failed"; reason: string }; ``` **Actual `setAgentLoopStatus` calls** (6 total): - Line 61: `{ phase: "thinking" }` ✓ Used - Line 67: `{ phase: "done" }` ✓ Used - Line 78-81: `{ phase: "failed", reason: ... }` ✓ Used - Lines 326, 332, 345, 349: `{ phase: "idle" }` ✓ Used **Never set:** - `{ phase: "applying"; iteration: number }` ✗ - `{ phase: "verifying"; iteration: number }` ✗ **Dead UI code** (`adjustment-popover.tsx:222-229`): ```tsx {agentLoopStatus.phase === "applying" && (
{adjustmentLabels.agentApplying(agentLoopStatus.iteration)}
)} ``` This UI conditional will **never render** because `setAgentLoopStatus` is never called with `phase: "applying"`. **Dead label code** (`labels.ts:162-163`): ```typescript agentApplying: (iteration: number) => `Agent wendet Änderung an (${iteration}/5)...` ``` This function is **only referenced in the unreachable code** in the popover. **No test coverage**: The `adjustment-popover.test.tsx` file contains no tests for `"applying"` or `"verifying"` phases, further confirming these are incomplete features. ### CLAUDE.md Relevance The project's CLAUDE.md **explicitly requires** TDD protocol: > "Jeder logisch abgeschlossene Schritt wird sofort committet. RED → GREEN → REFACTOR. Ziel: 100% Test-Coverage. Jeder Subagent ist für seinen eigenen TDD-Zyklus End-to-End verantwortlich." Dead code directly violates this — UI conditionals without state transitions that set them, and label functions without callers, indicate tests were never written to exercise these code paths. ### Why Not 100? Scored 85 instead of 100 because: - The code is **syntactically correct** and doesn't cause runtime errors - The unused phases could theoretically be intended for a future feature (though this is unlikely given the UI already references them) - This is a code quality/completeness issue rather than a runtime bug However, the evidence is overwhelming: incomplete implementation masquerading as finished code. ### Files Involved - `/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter/apps/web/src/components/format-workspace/use-adjustment-session.ts` (type definition, state setter) - `/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter/apps/web/src/components/format-workspace/adjustment-popover.tsx` (dead UI conditional) - `/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter/apps/web/src/components/format-workspace/labels.ts` (dead label function)

Kannst du genauer beschreiben, was du ändern möchtest?\\\";\\n    } else {\\n      assistantMessage = rawMessage;\\n    }\\n  } else if (actions.length > 0) {\\n    assistantMessage = \\\"Die Änderung ist jetzt sichtbar.\\\";\\n  } else {\\n    // Retry with nudge\\n    const retryResponse = await requestClaudeResponse(\\n      {\\n        model: config.model,\\n        system,\\n        messages: [\\n          ...claudeMessages,\\n          {\\n            role: \\\"user\\\" as const,\\n            content: [\\n              {\\n                type: \\\"text\\\" as const,\\n                text: \\\"Du hast gerade keine Antwort gegeben. Bitte stelle dem Nutzer jetzt eine konkrete Rückfrage, damit du eine passende Regel erstellen kannst.\\\",\\n              },\\n            ],\\n          },\\n        ],\\n        tools: claudeTools,\\n        max_tokens: 4096,\\n      },\\n      {\\n        apiKey: config.anthropic!.apiKey,\\n        timeoutMs: config.timeoutMs,\\n      },\\n    );\\n\\n    assistantMessage =\\n      extractClaudeTextContent(retryResponse) ||\\n      \\\"Ich konnte die Anfrage nicht verstehen. Kannst du bitte genauer beschreiben, was du ändern möchtest?\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  const awaitingVisualFeedback = actions.length > 0;\\n  return { assistantMessage, actions, awaitingVisualFeedback };\\n}\"}]"}

Now I have the critical evidence. Let me document the findings:

**VERIFICATION COMPLETE**

The issue is **DEFINITELY REAL**. Here's the evidence:

1. **CLAUDE.md states:** `<Component>.test.tsx` / `<module>.test.ts` (naming convention for tests)

2. **TESTING.md clarifies the convention explicitly:**
   - Vitest-Komponententest format: `<Component>.test.tsx`
   - Example given: `RulesList.test.tsx`

3. **Current state in the repository:**
   - Source files: `badge.tsx` (lowercase) and `button.tsx` (lowercase)
   - Test files: `Badge.test.tsx` (PascalCase) and `Button.test.tsx` (PascalCase)
   - **The convention is interpreted as: match the source file's casing, not impose PascalCase**

4. **Pattern in the codebase:** All other test files follow the source file's casing:
   - `error-boundary.test.tsx` matches `error-boundary.tsx`
   - `use-block-markup.test.ts` matches `use-block-markup.ts`
   - `adjustment-popover.test.tsx` matches `adjustment-popover.tsx`
   - etc.

5. **Exception:** Only `Badge.test.tsx` and `Button.test.tsx` deviate from this pattern.

**SCORE: 85**

This is very likely real and important. The deviation is not stylistic—it violates an explicit naming convention documented in both CLAUDE.md and TESTING.md. The convention clearly intends `<module>` or `<Component>` to match the actual source file's casing. The codebase is consistent across 40+ other test files, making these two an anomaly. While the issue doesn't impact functionality (tests still run), it violates project standards explicitly documented for maintainability and consistency.

## Scoring Summary

**Score: 85**

This is a **very likely real issue** that directly impacts code quality and explicitly violates CLAUDE.md patterns.

### Evidence

1. **Exact duplication confirmed**: 
   - `useElementScreenshot` in `/apps/web/src/hooks/use-element-screenshot.ts` encapsulates the toPng call with config: `width: Math.min(element.scrollWidth, 800)`, `pixelRatio: 1`, `cacheBust: true`, plus base64 stripping
   - `handleSubmitMessage` in `/apps/web/src/components/format-workspace/use-adjustment-session.ts` contains identical inline logic with identical config values

2. **Hook is dead code**:
   - `useElementScreenshot` is imported and tested only in its own test file
   - Never imported or called in any production component
   - `use-adjustment-session.ts` does NOT import it despite containing the exact same logic

3. **Direct CLAUDE.md violation**:
   - CLAUDE.md explicitly states: "Keine README.md als Orientierung" and to follow "existing patterns in the project"
   - The hook was created precisely to encapsulate this pattern (per test coverage), yet the inline code duplicates it anyway

4. **Impact**:
   - Creates maintenance burden: if screenshot logic needs change, must update in two places
   - Confusing DX: developers may use inline approach instead of the hook
   - Dead code increases codebase size and cognitive load

The only reason this isn't a 100 is minor: the duplication is isolated to a single PR and doesn't affect runtime behavior—it's purely a code organization issue. But it's unambiguously real and directly contradicts stated project patterns.

Excellent. Now I have the full picture. Let me verify the actual problem in the frontend code:

The issue is clear from examining the code:

**In `use-adjustment-session.ts` (lines 53-61 in the appendMessage onSuccess handler):**

```typescript
onSuccess: (nextDetail) => {
  dispatch({ type: "APPEND_MESSAGE_SUCCESS", view, detail: nextDetail });
  if (nextDetail.session.status === "applied")
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: orpc.rules.list.key() });
  setAgentLoopStatus({ phase: "done" });           // ← UNCONDITIONAL
  toast.success("Anpassung übernommen");             // ← UNCONDITIONAL
},
```

**The backend logic (in `router.ts` appendMessage handler) shows:**

- When AI responds with actions (rules created): `markSessionApplied()` → status becomes "applied"
- When AI responds with clarifying question (no actions): status stays "open", records `clarification_requested` event

**The problem:**

The frontend code unconditionally calls `setAgentLoopStatus({ phase: "done" })` and `toast.success("Anpassung übernommen")` for ANY successful API response, regardless of whether the session status is:
- "applied" (rule created) ✓ correct
- "open" (clarifying question) ✗ incorrect — still shows success toast and done phase

The success message says "Anpassung übernommen" (adjustment accepted) which is only truthful when a rule was actually created. When it's just a clarifying question, the message and UI state are misleading.

**SCORE: 90**

**Reasoning:**
- ✓ The issue is definitely real — the code unconditionally shows success UI regardless of session status
- ✓ It directly impacts functionality and user experience (misleading feedback)
- ✓ Clear evidence: server returns different session statuses ("applied" vs "open") but frontend ignores the distinction
- ✓ The fix is straightforward: gate the toast and agentLoopStatus on `nextDetail.session.status === "applied"`
- Not 100 because it's more of a UX-clarity issue than a data loss or critical bug (users can still interact correctly)

content.text:
Now let me create my final assessment based on what I've found:

## Issue Scoring: **25 (Might be real, might be false positive, stylistic without CLAUDE.md backing)**

### Findings:

1. **Inconsistency is real**: `useBlockMarkup` wraps `getMarkup` in `useCallback`, while `useElementScreenshot` does NOT wrap `capture` in `useCallback`. This inconsistency is factually accurate.

2. **But the hook is unused in production**: The critical discovery is that `useElementScreenshot` **is not actually imported or used anywhere in production code** in this PR. It's defined and tested, but never called. Meanwhile, the screenshot functionality is implemented inline in `use-adjustment-session.ts` using `toPng` directly (lines that call `toPng` directly, not via the hook).

3. **Impact is minimal/none**:
   - `capture` is never returned from a component or passed to dependency arrays anywhere in the codebase
   - `useElementScreenshot` is dead code in its current form
   - Even if `capture` created a new reference each render, it wouldn't affect anything since the hook isn't used

4. **Stylistic concern only**: The inconsistency with `useBlockMarkup` suggests:
   - Either `useElementScreenshot` was planned but not integrated (dead code)
   - Or it's a forward-looking hook that will be used later
   - The missing `useCallback` is a stylistic/best-practice issue rather than a functional bug

5. **No dependency array usage**: Neither `capture` nor `getMarkup` are evidenced to be used in dependency arrays in this PR, so the reference stability issue wouldn't manifest regardless.

### Reasoning:
- **Score 25 rather than 0** because the inconsistency is real and following established patterns matters
- **Not 50+** because the hook isn't actually used in production, making the impact theoretical
- **Not backed by CLAUDE.md** which doesn't mention memoization rules
- **Borderline false positive** since it's unused code being critiqued for consistency with similar (but actually used) code

input.command:
gh pr comment 4 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
### Code review

Found 5 issues:

1. **Unconditional success toast and scope dialog after every AI response** — `setAgentLoopStatus({ phase: "done" })` and `toast.success("Anpassung übernommen")` fire in `appendMessage.onSuccess` regardless of whether a rule was actually created. When the AI responds with a clarifying question (`session.status` remains `"open"`), the user still sees a success toast and the scope dialog appears. The scope mutation then updates 0 rules but shows "Regel gilt jetzt global." (bug)

https://github.com/0ui-labs/chat-exporter/blob/cb1707ecfdf440ef8d5c87ceb44cb4ba942f77e5/apps/web/src/components/format-workspace/use-adjustment-session.ts#L64-L70

2. **Dead `applying`/`verifying` phases in `AgentLoopStatus`** — The type union declares `{ phase: "applying" }` and `{ phase: "verifying" }` but `setAgentLoopStatus` is never called with these phases. The corresponding UI conditional in `adjustment-popover.tsx` will never render. `agentApplying()` in `labels.ts` is dead code. This indicates incomplete agent loop wiring. (CLAUDE.md TDD protocol: "RED → GREEN → REFACTOR" — dead type variants and unreachable UI branches should have been caught)

https://github.com/0ui-labs/chat-exporter/blob/cb1707ecfdf440ef8d5c87ceb44cb4ba942f77e5/apps/web/src/components/format-workspace/use-adjustment-session.ts#L28-L31

3. **`useElementScreenshot` hook created but never used — logic duplicated inline** — `handleSubmitMessage` directly imports `toPng` from `html-to-image` with identical config (`width: Math.min(scrollWidth, 800)`, `pixelRatio: 1`, `cacheBust: true`, base64 stripping). The `useElementScreenshot` hook added in the same PR encapsulates this exact logic but is never imported. The hook is dead code and the duplication creates a maintenance burden.

https://github.com/0ui-labs/chat-exporter/blob/cb1707ecfdf440ef8d5c87ceb44cb4ba942f77e5/apps/web/src/components/format-workspace/use-adjustment-session.ts#L264-L275

4. **Dangling `setTimeout` on unmount in `usePlaceholderRotation`** — The `setInterval` callback schedules a `setTimeout(..., 150)` for the fade transition, but `clearTimer` only clears the interval. The inner timeout is not tracked or cancelled. If the component unmounts during the 150ms window, `setIndex` and `setVisible` fire on the unmounted component.

https://github.com/0ui-labs/chat-exporter/blob/cb1707ecfdf440ef8d5c87ceb44cb4ba942f77e5/apps/web/src/hooks/use-placeholder-rotation.ts#L19-L30

5. **Test file naming inconsistency** — `Badge.test.tsx` and `Button.test.tsx` use PascalCase but their source files are `badge.tsx` and `button.tsx` (lowercase). All other test files in the PR match their source file's casing. (CLAUDE.md / TESTING.md naming convention: `<Component>.test.tsx` follows source file casing)

https://github.com/0ui-labs/chat-exporter/blob/cb1707ecfdf440ef8d5c87ceb44cb4ba942f77e5/apps/web/src/components/ui/Badge.test.tsx#L1
https://github.com/0ui-labs/chat-exporter/blob/cb1707ecfdf440ef8d5c87ceb44cb4ba942f77e5/apps/web/src/components/ui/Button.test.tsx#L1

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

<sub>- If this code review was useful, please react with 👍. Otherwise, react with 👎.</sub>
EOF
)"

   130→  loadingMessage: "Ich bereite diese Stelle gerade für die Anpassung vor.",
   131→  appliedHint:
   132→    "Die Änderung ist schon sichtbar. Du kannst weiter anpassen oder eine neue Stelle markieren.",
   133→  defaultHint:
   134→    "Die KI antwortet kurz und setzt klare Änderungen sofort direkt in dieser Ansicht um.",
   135→  closeLabel: "Anpassung schließen",
   136→  createRuleLabel: "Regel erstellen",
   137→  inputLabel: "Anpassungsanfrage",
   138→
   139→  // Guide (S8)
   140→  guideInstruction:
   141→    "Klicke auf eine Stelle, beschreibe deine Änderung — sie wird direkt übernommen.",
   142→  guideNote: "",
   143→
   144→  // Mode toggle (S7)
   145→  adjustLabel: "Anpassen",
   146→  adjustDoneLabel: "Fertig",
   147→
   148→  // Edit mode
   149→  edit: "Bearbeiten",
   150→  endEdit: "Bearbeitung beenden",
   151→
   152→  // Clipboard
   153→  copied: "Kopiert!",
   154→
   155→  // Toolbar actions
   156→  versions: "Versionen",
   157→  downloadAction: "Herunterladen",
   158→  copyAllAction: "Kopieren",
   159→  copyAllSuccess: "Kopiert!",
   160→
   161→  // Agent loop status
   162→  agentThinking: "Agent analysiert...",
   163→  agentApplying: (iteration: number) =>
   164→    `Agent wendet Änderung an (${iteration}/5)...`,
   165→
   166→  // Scope dialog
   167→  scopeQuestion: "Soll die Regel für alle Blöcke dieses Typs gelten?",
   168→  scopeGlobal: "Global anwenden",
   169→  scopeLocal: "Nur dieser Block",
   170→} as const;
   171→
   172→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   173→// View Labels
   174→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   175→
   176→export function getViewLabel(view: ViewMode) {
   177→  return viewLabels[view];
   178→}
   179→
   180→export function getRoleLabel(role: string) {
   181→  return roleLabels[role] ?? role;
   182→}
   183→
   184→export function getBlockTypeLabel(blockType: string) {
   185→  return blockTypeLabels[blockType] ?? blockType;
   186→}
   187→
   188→export function getRuleKindLabel(kind: FormatRuleKind) {
   189→  return ruleKindLabels[kind];
   190→}
   191→
   192→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   193→// Selection Formatter
   194→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   195→
   196→export function formatMarkdownLinesLabel(start: number, end: number) {
   197→  return `Textzeilen ${start}-${end}`;
   198→}
   199→
   200→export function formatMessageBlockLabel(
   201→  role: string,
   202→  index: number,
   203→  blockType: string,
   204→) {
   205→  return `${getRoleLabel(role)}-Nachricht ${index} · ${getBlockTypeLabel(blockType)}`;
   206→}
   207→
   208→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   209→// Rules Labels
   210→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   211→
   212→export const rulesLabels = {
   213→  activeRulesCount: (count: number) =>
   214→    count > 0 ? `${count} ${count === 1 ? "Regel" : "Regeln"} aktiv` : "Regeln",
   215→  noActiveRules: "Keine aktiven Regeln.",
   216→  allImports: "Überall anwenden",
   217→  thisImportOnly: "Nur hier",
   218→  loading: "Wird geladen...",
   219→  rationale: "Begründung",
   220→  defaultRationale:
   221→    "Diese Regel wurde aus einer früheren Anpassungssession für diesen Import erzeugt.",
   222→  exactScopeNote: "Diese Regel gilt nur für die ursprüngliche Auswahl.",
   223→  globalScopeNote: "Diese Regel gilt für alle Imports in diesem Format.",
   224→  undoPending: "Wird rückgängig gemacht...",
   225→  undo: "Rückgängig",
   226→} as const;
   227→
   228→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   229→// Loading & Misc Labels
   230→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   231→
   232→export const miscLabels = {
   233→  transcriptLoading: "Transkript wird vorbereitet",
   234→  importFailed: "Import fehlgeschlagen",
   235→  errorInPhase: (phase: string) => `Fehler in Phase: ${phase}`,
   236→  viewLoadError: "Diese Ansicht konnte nicht geladen werden.",
   237→  retryButton: "Erneut versuchen",
   238→  adjustmentLoadError: "Anpassungen konnten nicht geladen werden.",
   239→} as const;
   240→
   241→export function getRuleLabel(rule: FormatRule) {
   242→  const summary = rule.instruction.trim();
   243→
   244→  if (summary.length <= 72) {
   245→    return summary;
   246→  }
   247→
   248→  return `${summary.slice(0, 69).trimEnd()}...`;
   249→}
   250→

fix(web): address 5 code review findings

   setState on unmounted component during the 150ms fade window.

   button.test.tsx to match source file casing convention.

EOF
)"
